intitule: Lesson 13: The Benefits of Volunteering (Reading & Grammar)

enonce.texte:
Document: The Hidden Rewards of Helping Others

Volunteering is often seen as a selfless act, but research shows that those who give their time to help others also gain significant benefits for themselves. When you volunteer, you are not just contributing to your community; you are investing in your own well-being. For instance, acquiring new skills through community service can boost your self-esteem and make you feel more competent. Furthermore, regular volunteers often report lower levels of stress and a greater sense of purpose. The social connections formed while working towards a common goal also help fight feelings of isolation. In a world that often focuses on individual success, volunteering reminds us of our shared humanity and creates a positive ripple effect that benefits everyone involved.
images: A group of diverse young adults in Cameroon, smiling and working together to plant trees in a community garden. One person is holding a sign that says 'Community First'. The background shows a clear sky and a few simple houses.

questions.texte: Identify the main idea of the text.
questions.reponse: The main idea is that volunteering provides significant benefits to the volunteers themselves, not just to the community.

questions.texte: List two personal benefits of volunteering mentioned in the text.
questions.reponse: Two personal benefits are: 1) boosting self-esteem / feeling more competent, and 2) reducing stress / finding a greater sense of purpose.

questions.texte: Explain the phrase 'positive ripple effect' as used in the context of the text.
questions.reponse: It means that a single act of volunteering creates a chain of positive consequences that spread outwards, benefiting more people than just the direct recipient of the help.

questions.texte: Complete the following sentences using the infinitive of purpose (to + verb).

sousQuestions.texte: Many young people join community service programs ____________________.
sousQuestions.reponse: to gain new skills / to help their community / to meet new people.

sousQuestions.texte: She started a recycling club in her neighborhood ____________________.
sousQuestions.reponse: to reduce waste / to protect the environment / to raise awareness.

questions.texte: Combine the two sentences into one sentence using an infinitive of purpose.

sousQuestions.texte: He volunteered at the local hospital. He wanted to comfort sick children.
sousQuestions.reponse: He volunteered at the local hospital to comfort sick children.

sousQuestions.texte: They organized a fundraising event. They intended to buy supplies for the orphanage.
sousQuestions.reponse: They organized a fundraising event to buy supplies for the orphanage.

intitule: Lesson 13: Applying for a Community Project

enonce.texte:
Real-Life Problem: The 'Clean Campus' Initiative

You are the President of the Science Club at your lycée in Yaoundé. Your club wants to launch a 'Clean Campus' initiative to address the growing problem of plastic waste. Your goal is to convince the school administration to support your project and to recruit 20 student volunteers.

Task 1: Persuasive Letter - Write a formal letter to the Principal. Explain the purpose of your initiative (to create a cleaner, healthier learning environment). Describe the actions you plan to take (e.g., installing recycling bins, organizing a monthly cleanup day). Explain what the students will gain from volunteering (skills, satisfaction).

Task 2: Recruitment Speech - Write a short speech (5-6 sentences) you will give during the morning assembly to persuade other students to join. Use at least three infinitives of purpose (e.g., "Join us to make a difference...").
images: A picture of a crowded schoolyard in Cameroon with plastic bottles and wrappers littering the ground next to a trash can that is overflowing. Students in uniforms are walking past.

questions.texte: Write the formal letter to the Principal as described in Task 1. Your letter must include: a clear subject line, an explanation of the problem, a description of your proposed solution, and the benefits for the students.
questions.reponse:
**Subject: Proposal for the 'Clean Campus' Initiative**

Dear Principal,

I am writing to you as the President of the Science Club to propose a new 'Clean Campus' initiative. The amount of plastic waste in our schoolyard has become a serious problem, affecting the beauty and hygiene of our learning environment.

To address this, our club plans to install three separate recycling bins for plastic, paper, and organic waste. Furthermore, we will organize a monthly 'Cleanup Friday' where volunteers will spend 30 minutes after class collecting and sorting litter.

Students who volunteer will gain valuable skills in project management and environmental science. Most importantly, they will work together to create a school they can be proud of. We respectfully request your permission to launch this initiative next month.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]
President, Science Club

questions.texte: Write the recruitment speech for the morning assembly as described in Task 2.
questions.reponse: Good morning, everyone! Look around our campus—the plastic waste is taking over. Join the Science Club's 'Clean Campus' initiative to transform our school into a place we can all be proud of. We need your energy to sort waste and clean up just one Friday a month. Volunteer with us to learn new skills, to protect our environment, and to prove that small actions create big changes. Sign up after class today!
